L1.area: Image metadata reading and parsing layer

L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) injection vulnerability exists due to an insecurely initialized XML parser used for reading image metadata. An attacker can supply a file with a malicious metadata segment, and when the image metadata is parsed, external entities are resolved, potentially enabling file disclosure or server-side request forgery.

L3.area: XMP metadata parsing within the image I/O metadata module
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) injection vulnerability exists due to an insecurely initialized XML parser used specifically for reading XMP metadata embedded in image files. An attacker can supply an image with a malicious XMP segment, and when the XMP metadata is parsed, external entities are resolved, potentially enabling file disclosure or server-side request forgery.

ground_truth.reason: The XML parser used to read XMP metadata from image files is initialized without disabling external entity resolution, allowing an attacker to trigger XXE injection by supplying an image with a crafted XMP metadata segment.
